title: Sideritis × angustifolia subsp. funkiana
what_description: Sideritis × angustifolia subsp. funkiana is a plant species belonging to the family Lamiaceae. It is a perennial herb with a woody base, growing up to 30-50 cm in height, and has linear-lanceolate leaves that are 2-4 cm long and 0.5-1.5 mm wide. The inflorescence is a terminal spike of small, tubular flowers that are 2-3 mm long and bear a 5-lobed calyx. The flowers are purple and are densely packed on the spike. This plant is native to the Iberian Peninsula and is characterized by its narrow, pointed leaves and small, purple flowers.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Sideritis × angustifolia subsp. funkiana are inflammation, which it reduces through its anti-inflammatory compounds, and respiratory problems, including asthma, due to its bronchodilatory effects. Digestive issues such as diarrhea are alleviated by its anti-diarrheal properties. Anxiety is treated through its anxiolytic action, while oxidative stress is mitigated by its antioxidant properties. Its expectorant properties help to relieve cough and fever, making it an effective treatment for respiratory infections. Additionally, it has analgesic, antiarthritic, and wound-healing properties, as well as being used to treat rheumatism, cardiovascular disease, hypertension, migraines, and insomnia, and also has anti-diabetic and anti-hypertensive effects.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Sideritis × angustifolia subsp. funkiana are antioxidant and antimicrobial, attributed to its content of flavonoids and volatile oils. Its anti-inflammatory activity is primarily due to the presence of saponins, which contribute to its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. As an analgesic, the plant's bioactive compounds help to relieve pain by modulating the nervous system. Additionally, its antispasmodic properties are a result of the plant's ability to relax smooth muscle, providing relief from spasms and cramps. The plant's therapeutic properties are further supported by its ability to exhibit antioxidant activity, protecting cells from oxidative damage.
constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Sideritis × angustifolia subsp. funkiana are rosmarinic acid, caffeic acid, quercetin, luteolin, and apigenin. These compounds possess potent antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which are thought to contribute to the plant's therapeutic effects. Rosmarinic acid and caffeic acid have been shown to exert anti-inflammatory activity by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, while quercetin has been demonstrated to possess antioxidant properties, scavenging free radicals and reducing oxidative stress. Luteolin and apigenin have been found to have antimicrobial activity against a range of microorganisms, including bacteria and fungi. The combination of these bioactive compounds in Sideritis × angustifolia subsp. funkiana may contribute to its traditional use in treating various health conditions, including respiratory and gastrointestinal disorders.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Sideritis × angustifolia subsp. funkiana are derived from its leaves and flowers, which are rich in flavonoids, terpenoids, and other bioactive compounds. Tea and infusion are traditional preparations made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water, which has been reported to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. Tincture is a concentrated liquid extract made by soaking the plant material in a solvent, often used for its sedative and anti-anxiety effects. Salve and capsules are other preparations that utilize the plant's bioactive compounds, with salve being applied topically to soothe skin irritations and capsules providing a standardized dosage for internal use. These preparations have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including insomnia, anxiety, and digestive issues.
side_effects_description: The possible side effects of using Sideritis × angustifolia subsp. funkiana improperly include experiencing allergic reactions, which can be attributed to the plant's ability to cause an immune system response in some individuals. The plant may also cause gastrointestinal issues, such as diarrhea and nausea, due to its potential to interact with the digestive system. Additionally, using the plant improperly may lead to dizziness and lightheadedness, which can be caused by its effects on blood pressure and circulation. These side effects can be exacerbated by the plant's potential to interact with other medications or health conditions, making it essential to use it under the guidance of a healthcare professional. Improper use of Sideritis × angustifolia subsp. funkiana may also lead to overstimulation and exacerbate pre-existing conditions, such as high blood pressure and anxiety disorders.
